top_signals.title: Half-Open, Half-Closed Strait of Hormuz Baffles Oil Markets
top_signals.summary: Oil prices have continued to whipsaw since the U.S. and Iran announced last week signed a deal to make a deal as traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is anything but straight and not expected to normalize within days. Contradictory messaging from Iran and the United States on the navigability of the chokepoint have intensified during the weekend amid a difficult start to the (delayed) talks in Switzerland. Shippers and insurers, who were already very cautious about returning to the Persian Gulf and its key trade lane, are wary of an operating environment…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: SpaceX Rout Continues as Tech Selloff Hits Stocks | The Pulse 6/23/2026
top_signals.summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Ludovic Subran, Allianz CIO & Chief Economist; Victoria Honeyman, University of Leeds Professor of British Politics; Antony Jenkins, 10x Banking CEO & Founder (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Government is set to deal major blow to Big Tech’s moves into sports rights
top_signals.summary: The government is set to deal a major blow to Big Tech’s moves into sports rights after launching a consultation on the future of broadcasting. A green paper – an exploratory consultation – was announced by the minister for creative industries, media and arts, Ian Murray, on Tuesday, proposing to prevent streaming platforms such as [...]
